project_type: Next.js 16 App Router task manager with Supabase backend
build_system: npm with Next.js built-in tooling. Node.js v22 available via `source /root/.nvm/nvm.sh` before running any node/npm commands.
test_framework: NONE currently. Will set up Jest with React Testing Library for component tests and server action unit tests.
verification_instructions: 1. Run `npm install` to install deps. 2. Run `npm test` to execute all tests. 3. All tests must pass with zero failures.
environment_constraints: Node.js v22 via nvm (must run `source /root/.nvm/nvm.sh` before any node/npm commands). OPEN_INTERNET network mode - can install any packages. No Supabase instance available - all Supabase calls must be mocked in tests.
contribution_requirements: AGENTS.md warns this is Next.js 16 with possible breaking changes from training data. Test framework must be compatible with React 19, Next.js 16, and TypeScript 5.
key_patterns: Server actions in app/actions.ts use `createClient()` from @/lib/supabase/server.ts. Components use `useTransition` and `useActionState` for async operations. Middleware uses @supabase/ssr directly. Path aliases use @/* mapping to project root.
directory_structure: app/ - Next.js App Router pages and components. app/actions.ts - main server actions (CRUD tasks + signOut). app/login/ and app/signup/ - auth pages with their own server actions. app/components/ - client components (AddTaskForm, TaskList). lib/supabase/ - Supabase client factory. middleware.ts - auth guard middleware.
